ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Iowa 2026 primary election (past). Polls were open 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM Central Time. IA-03 Democratic primary was uncontested — Sarah Trone Garriott is the presumptive Democratic nominee pending SOS certification.
Pre-registration deadline for June 2 primary (past). Same-day registration was available May 19 through June 2 with photo ID and proof of residence.
Mailed-absentee request deadline for June 2 primary (past). In-person absentee voting was available through June 1.
Primary absentee ballot receipt deadline (past). Ballots had to be received by 8:00 PM CT on election day; postmarks did not count.
Iowa 2026 general election. Polls open 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM Central Time. Presumptive matchup: Rep. Zach Nunn (R) vs. Sarah Trone Garriott (D).
Pre-registration deadline for November 3 general election (15 days before, by 5:00 PM CT). Same-day registration available Oct 20 through Nov 3 with proof of ID and residence.
Deadline for mailed absentee ballot requests to reach the county auditor by 5:00 PM CT. In-person absentee voting available through November 2.
Absentee ballots must be received by the county auditor by 8:00 PM CT on election day. Postmarks do not count.

ELECTION_EVENTS.milestone: Last day for voters to fix incomplete affidavit envelopes
ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Voters have until the time polls close at 8:00 p.m. CT to fix incomplete affidavit envelopes at the county auditor's office.
Iowa holds a closed primary. Voters must be registered with a party to participate. Independents may change party registration on election day at the polls. IA-03 Democratic primary was uncontested.

RULES.currentRule: Online, in person, or by mail. Must be a U.S. citizen, Iowa resident, 18+ by election day. Pre-registration deadline is 15 days before the election. Same-day registration is available through election day with photo ID and proof of residence.
RULES.whyItMatters: Determines who can vote in IA-03. Voters in all 21 counties — including Polk (Des Moines) and Dallas — must register with their county auditor.
RULES.detail: General pre-reg deadline: October 19, 2026 at 5 PM CT. Same-day registration available Oct 20 – Nov 3 with photo ID + proof of current address.

RULES.currentRule: Iowa HF 954 (signed June 2025) strengthens citizenship verification at voter registration — cross-referencing DOT noncitizen ID files. Registrants flagged as potential non-citizens are placed in 'unconfirmed' status and must provide documentation to vote a full ballot.
RULES.whyItMatters: New 2025 law adds an active citizenship cross-check at registration. Polk County's large and diverse population may see elevated 'unconfirmed' designations — monitor for implementation issues.
RULES.detail: Iowa HF 954 took effect in 2025. Polk County has Iowa's largest voter rolls — verify county auditor implementation guidance before the general election.

RULES.currentRule: Iowa requires photo ID to vote in person. Acceptable IDs: Iowa driver's license, Iowa non-operator ID, U.S. passport, military/veteran ID, tribal ID, or Iowa Voter ID card (must be signed). ID must not be expired more than 90 days for driver's licenses.
RULES.whyItMatters: Voters without a qualifying ID will need another registered voter in the same precinct to attest to their identity, or they may cast a provisional ballot.
RULES.detail: Iowa Voter ID cards are issued automatically to registered voters who lack an Iowa driver's license or non-operator ID. Contact the Polk or Dallas county auditor to request one.

RULES.currentRule: Any registered voter may request an absentee ballot — no excuse required. Request must include Iowa driver's license number, non-operator ID number, voter ID/verification number, or 4-digit PIN from voter ID card.
RULES.whyItMatters: Absentee voting is widely used in Polk County and suburban IA-03 communities. Requests must be submitted by the county auditor deadline; ballots must be received — not postmarked — by 8 PM on election day.

RULES.currentRule: Mailed absentee ballot requests must be received by the county auditor by 5:00 PM, 15 days before the election. General deadline: October 19, 2026. In-person requests may be made through the day before the election.
RULES.whyItMatters: Late mailed requests are rejected. Voters who miss the mail deadline may still request an absentee ballot in person at the county auditor's office through the day before the election.

RULES.currentRule: In-person absentee voting begins 20 days before the election at each county auditor's office. General window: October 14 – November 2, 2026.
RULES.whyItMatters: Iowa's early voting mechanism is the 'in-person absentee' system — voters visit the county auditor's office, request and cast their ballot in one step. Polk County may offer additional satellite sites.

RULES.currentRule: Polls are open 7:00 AM to 8:00 PM Central Time on election day. Any voter in line at 8:00 PM is entitled to vote. Iowa does not use open vote centers — voters must go to their assigned precinct.
RULES.whyItMatters: IA-03 voters must vote at their assigned precinct. In Polk County, with many precincts, voters should confirm their polling place before election day.

RULES.currentRule: Iowa holds a closed primary — voters must be registered with a party to vote in that party's primary. Unaffiliated voters must declare a party affiliation to participate. Party registration can be changed on election day.
RULES.whyItMatters: IA-03's Democratic primary was uncontested — Sarah Trone Garriott is the presumptive nominee. The November general matchup is now the active contest.

RULES.currentRule: Absentee ballots must be physically received by the county auditor by 8:00 PM on election day. Postmarks do not count. General receipt deadline: November 3, 2026 at 8 PM CT.
RULES.whyItMatters: Most common cause of absentee ballot rejection in Iowa. Voters in suburban and rural IA-03 counties should return ballots by drop-off at the county auditor rather than relying on mail transit.
RULES.detail: Iowa Code § 53. Ballots may be returned in person to the county auditor by any person authorized by the voter, including a family member.

RULES.currentRule: Common rejection causes: late receipt, missing or invalid voter verification number on request form, signature mismatch, voter not registered at address on file.
RULES.whyItMatters: Rejected absentee ballots are not counted. Iowa does not have a broad cure process — voters should confirm ballot receipt with the county auditor before election day.

RULES.currentRule: Voters who lack required ID, are not in the precinct roster, or whose registration is in 'unconfirmed' or 'inactive' status may cast a provisional ballot. Counted after identity and eligibility are confirmed.
RULES.whyItMatters: Affects IA-03 voters who lack ID, have recently moved (Polk County has high residential mobility), or face registration issues under HF 954 citizenship-verification rules.

RULES.currentRule: County canvass held within 10 days after the election. State canvass and certification occurs not later than 27 days after election day (Iowa Code § 50.38).
RULES.whyItMatters: Determines when IA-03 race results become official. Polk County's large vote count typically drives statewide timelines.
RULES.detail: General election state canvass: not later than December 1, 2026 (Iowa Code § 50.38(c)).

RULES.currentRule: Iowa HF 928 (2025): recount may be requested for federal offices if margin < 0.15%. Recounts must use election-day tabulation machines, cover all precincts, and be conducted by county auditors with bipartisan boards. Secretary of State may take over a recount if the board does not follow the law.
RULES.whyItMatters: IA-03 has been competitive in recent cycles (Nunn won 2024 with 51.8%). The 0.15% threshold and SOS takeover authority are new 2025 provisions that could be triggered in a close race.
RULES.detail: HF 928 signed June 2, 2025. Recount requests may be filed within 3 days of county canvass. State covers cost if margin is within 0.15% for federal races. Completion deadline: 18 days after county canvass.

RESOURCES.label: Voter Ready Iowa — Official voter guide

RESOURCES.label: Register to vote in Iowa
RESOURCES.description: Online voter registration through the Iowa DOT portal, or download the paper form for county auditor submission.

RESOURCES.description: Official information on requesting and returning absentee ballots, including deadlines and the voter verification number requirement.

UPDATES.title: HF 928 recount reform now in effect for 2026
UPDATES.fieldChanged: Recount eligibility threshold for federal offices
UPDATES.oldValue: No automatic recount; candidate-requested with no statutory margin threshold
UPDATES.newValue: Recount may be requested if margin < 0.15% for federal races (HF 928, eff. July 1, 2025)

UPDATES.title: IA-03 Democratic primary uncontested — Trone Garriott is presumptive nominee
UPDATES.newValue: Sarah Trone Garriott (D, state senator) is the presumptive Democratic nominee for November 3 general

UPDATES.title: Dashboard pivot: primary deadlines past, general-election focus active
UPDATES.oldValue: KPI cards led with primary registration and absentee deadlines (May 18, June 2)
UPDATES.newValue: KPI cards now lead with general election deadlines (Oct 19 registration, Nov 3 election day, ballot receipt by 8 PM CT)

UPDATES.title: Libertarian Marco Battaglia struck from IA-03 general-election ballot
UPDATES.oldValue: November 3 general-election matchup recorded as Nunn (R) vs. Trone Garriott (D); third-party status not separately tracked.
UPDATES.newValue: In mid-June 2026, the Iowa State Objection Panel struck Libertarian candidate Marco Battaglia from the IA-03 general-election ballot over a signature-petition challenge. Battaglia has publicly said he intends to keep campaigning and is weighing a legal challenge. Separately, Politico and CBS2 Iowa reported on June 15–16 that Battaglia alleges Republican operatives, including outreach he attributed to Robert F. Kennedy Jr., urged him to drop out of the race. Rep. Zach Nunn's campaign denies coordinating any such pressure. The Nunn (R) vs. Trone Garriott (D) two-way race remains the operative November 3 matchup pending any successful Battaglia court challenge.

UPDATES.title: General-election deadlines re-checked; no changes detected
UPDATES.oldValue: Deadlines confirmed as of 2026-06-16 bi-weekly refresh.
UPDATES.newValue: As of the June 17, 2026 refresh, Iowa Secretary of State and Polk County auditor guidance still list October 19, 2026 (5:00 PM CT) as the pre-registration deadline, October 19, 2026 (5:00 PM CT) as the mailed-absentee-request deadline, October 14 – November 2, 2026 as the in-person early-voting window, and November 3, 2026 at 8:00 PM CT as Election Day and the absentee-ballot receipt deadline. Postmarks do not count.

CONFLICTS.factInQuestion: Polk County in-person absentee satellite sites for 2026 general election
CONFLICTS.sourceAClaim: Statewide rule: in-person absentee voting at county auditor's office begins 20 days before election.
CONFLICTS.sourceBClaim: Polk County may operate additional satellite absentee voting sites beyond the auditor's office — specific sites for 2026 not yet confirmed.
CONFLICTS.whyItMatters: Des Moines / Polk County voters may have access to additional early voting locations not captured in statewide guidance.
CONFLICTS.suggestedReviewAction: Re-check Polk County Auditor website for satellite absentee voting locations announcement before October 1, 2026 (and confirm before general-election early voting opens October 14).

NEWS.headline: Iowa Libertarian says Republicans urged him to drop out of IA-03 race
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Marco Battaglia, the Libertarian candidate just struck from the IA-03 ballot by the Iowa State Objection Panel, told CBS2 Iowa that Republican operatives — including outreach he attributed to Robert F. Kennedy Jr. — pressured him to withdraw. Rep. Zach Nunn's campaign denies any coordinated pressure. Battaglia says he intends to keep campaigning and is weighing a legal challenge to the ballot ruling.

NEWS.headline: Battleground Iowa House race takes bizarre turn with Libertarian challenger
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Politico reports on the Iowa State Objection Panel removing Libertarian Marco Battaglia from the IA-03 general-election ballot and on Battaglia's allegation that Republican-aligned figures, including Robert F. Kennedy Jr., pressed him to drop out of the Nunn–Trone Garriott race. Nunn's team denies any pressure campaign. Battaglia says he will continue campaigning and may pursue court action.

NEWS.headline: 2026 Iowa primary election results
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Iowa Public Radio's statewide June 2 primary results roundup. IA-03 does not appear in the results tables because both Rep. Zach Nunn (R) and state Sen. Sarah Trone Garriott (D) ran unopposed in their respective primaries — the November 3 general election matchup is set.

NEWS.headline: Iowa primary election day: Nunn and Trone Garriott both uncontested, November rematch set
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Both Rep. Zach Nunn (R) and state Sen. Sarah Trone Garriott (D) are running unopposed in the June 2 IA-03 primary. The November 3 general election matchup is already set. IA-03 is rated Lean Republican but among Democrats' top national targets.

NEWS.headline: Uncontested primary sets stage for Iowa's highly targeted 3rd Congressional District race
NEWS.relevanceSummary: IA-03's Democratic primary is uncontested, making the November 3 general election the key contest. Presumptive matchup: Rep. Zach Nunn (R) vs. state Sen. Sarah Trone Garriott (D). The race is among Democrats' top national targets given Nunn's 51.8% 2024 margin.

NEWS.headline: Democratic state Sen. Sarah Trone Garriott announces run for Rep. Nunn's seat
NEWS.relevanceSummary: State Sen. Sarah Trone Garriott of West Des Moines officially launched her campaign for IA-03. She represents the 14th Senate district (2021–present). Jennifer Konfrst, Iowa House minority leader, endorsed Trone Garriott. The race is rated Lean Republican / competitive by analysts.

NEWS.headline: New poll shows Rep. Zach Nunn trailing two Democratic candidates in IA-03
NEWS.relevanceSummary: A November 2025 poll showed incumbent Rep. Nunn trailing potential Democratic challengers in IA-03, reinforcing the district's status as a top Democratic pickup target. Nunn won by 51.8% in 2024 against Democrat Lanon Baccam.

NEWS.headline: Iowa recount process gets major overhaul under HF 928
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Iowa's new recount law (HF 928, signed June 2025) sets a 0.15% margin threshold for federal race recounts, requires all-precinct machine recounts, and gives the Secretary of State authority to take over a recount if county boards don't follow the law — relevant for any close IA-03 race.

NEWS.headline: Iowa bans ranked-choice voting, strengthens citizenship checks under HF 954
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Gov. Reynolds signed HF 954 banning ranked-choice voting statewide and requiring citizenship verification at voter registration through DOT database cross-checks. The Brennan Center flagged Iowa's new SOS recount-takeover authority as among 2025's most significant election administration laws.

LAST_REFRESH.summary: Automated refresh completed 2026-07-08 for IA-03. Sources checked: 23. LLM verifications: 24. 1 lastVerified bump on unchanged sources.

WEEKLY_REPORT.summary: Weekly refresh for June 15–17, 2026. Headline development for IA-03 this cycle: the Iowa State Objection Panel struck Libertarian Marco Battaglia from the November ballot; Battaglia is publicly contesting and alleges Republican-aligned pressure (including outreach attributed to RFK Jr.) urging him to withdraw — per Politico and CBS2 Iowa, June 15–16. Nunn campaign denies any coordinated pressure. Nunn (R) vs. Trone Garriott (D) remains the operative two-way matchup pending court action. Re-checked Iowa SOS and Polk County calendars — general-election deadlines unchanged (Oct. 19 registration, Oct. 19 mail-absentee request, Oct. 14 – Nov. 2 early voting, Nov. 3 Election Day and 8:00 PM CT receipt). Watch items unchanged: HF 928 recount reform (0.15% federal threshold; SOS takeover authority) and HF 954 citizenship verification both still on 'watch' with needsReview:true. Polk County satellite absentee sites still not announced — conflict-ia03-001 remains open, next review by Oct 1, 2026. Added news-ia03-008 (Politico) and news-ia03-009 (CBS2 Iowa) for Battaglia coverage. No confidence flips — all rules await owner verification before publish.
